originalAlarm.description: Value stored to 'pid_output' is never read

presenceCondition:
(or (and MARLIN_H (not PIDTEMP) (not PID_DEBUG))
    (and (not MARLIN_H) (not PIDTEMP) (not PID_DEBUG))
    (and MARLIN_H (not PIDTEMP) PID_DEBUG)
    (and (not MARLIN_H) (not PIDTEMP) PID_DEBUG))
model:
(define-fun PID_DEBUG () Bool
  true)
(define-fun MARLIN_H () Bool
  false)
(define-fun PIDTEMP () Bool
  false)
reasoning: The known VBDB bug for 8c4377dd635.c is at L16 (SERIAL_ECHO_START called when MARLIN_H=T makes the macro undefined). This alarm is about pid_output=0 at L19 (in the \!PIDTEMP branch) being written but never subsequently read — a genuine dead store. This is a different, new bug not in the VBDB. The PC simplifies to \!PIDTEMP (all 4 clauses cover all combos of MARLIN_H and PID_DEBUG with \!PIDTEMP). MARLIN_H and PID_DEBUG do not affect whether this dead store occurs, so \!PIDTEMP is the exact condition. Correctly constrained.
